festivals.name: Buenos Aires Tango Festival
festivals.description: The world's premier tango event fills Buenos Aires with milongas, shows, and the world championship finals.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional de la Vendimia
festivals.description: Argentina's spectacular grape harvest festival in Mendoza's wine country with night parades and the crowning of the Harvest Queen.

destinations.name: Buenos Aires
destinations.description: The Paris of South America captivates with European architecture, world-class steakhouses, and the birthplace of tango.

destinations.name: Patagonia (Bariloche)
destinations.description: Switzerland of Argentina: a chocolate-box lake district ringed by Andean peaks, with skiing in winter and trekking in summer.

destinations.name: Iguazú Falls
destinations.description: The world's largest waterfall system — 275 falls across nearly 3km — on the Argentina-Brazil border.

destinations.name: Mendoza Wine Region
destinations.description: The world capital of Malbec wine sits in the shadow of the Andes where bodegas offer tours and the highest vines in the world.

destinations.name: El Calafate & Perito Moreno
destinations.description: Perito Moreno is one of the world's only advancing glaciers — you can walk on it and watch house-sized icebergs calve.

destinations.name: Torres del Paine (Patagonia)
destinations.description: Three granite towers soar above turquoise lakes in Chile's most dramatic national park, accessible from El Calafate.

destinations.name: Salta & the Northwest
destinations.description: Argentina's Andean northwest has colonial Salta, the rainbow-coloured Quebrada de Humahuaca, and high-altitude wine.

destinations.name: Península Valdés
destinations.description: A UNESCO reserve where southern right whales breach, orcas beach-hunt sea lions, and Magellanic penguins nest.
